_comment: CENTRAL, PROGRAMMATIC SOURCE OF TRUTH for every tag namespace. Consumed by the Python tagging pipeline (scripts/enrich_cases.py stamps each emitted tag with its source + authority from here) and by the viewer (renders 'tentative' tiers as dotted pills). When more than one source tags the same case in the same namespace, the HIGHEST-authority source wins; a tag is shown 'tentative' (dotted) when its only available source is tentative-tier. Keep this file as the one place tag provenance/weighing is defined.

tentative.from: Tentative ruling text — the only data available until docket/OCR capture runs. Inferred, low-recall, lowest authority.

docket.from: Case-info docket: Payments / Register of Actions / Parties / Attorneys. Court-confirmed facts; authoritative for categories, parties, financials, fee waivers.

ocr.from: OCR'd complaint caption — the full set of pleaded causes of action. Most complete for 'cause'.

judicial_council.from: Statewide Judicial Council of California Vexatious Litigant List (CCP 391.7 prefiling orders; https://courts.ca.gov/system/files/file/vexlit.pdf), updated MONTHLY. The authoritative 'currently in effect' roster for vexatious-litigant status; carries the issuing court + case number + order date, so SF rows match our cases by case_number (strong).

_authority_note: Authority (weighing) is NOT the same as the tier number. docket(3) > ocr(2) > tentative(1). For factual namespaces (category/litigant/financial) the docket is the source of truth; for the full cause-of-action list the OCR caption is the source of truth; tentative is always the fallback of last resort.

matter.notes: Matter type (real-property, probate, unlawful-detainer, ...). Docket Parties/category authoritative; tentative inferred from caption/ruling.

category.notes: The docket's official 'Cause of Action' field (usually ONE per case). Parent of the 'cause' namespace.

cause.notes: Specific causes of action. OCR'd complaint caption is the complete pleaded list (source of truth); rendered as child sub-tags under the docket 'category'. Tentative inference is the fallback.

litigant.notes: individual vs entity. Docket Parties tab authoritative; tentative parses the caption.

fee_waiver.notes: Indigent / in-forma-pauperis court-fee waiver. Source of truth is the docket ROA/Payments (request -> grant/deny -> withdraw-on-improved-situation/settlement -> itemized repayment of waived fees; fixture CGC16556148). Tentative-text detection (~50 cases) is a low-recall, dotted fallback until docket capture runs.

outcome.notes: Disposition/outcome tags derived from tentative-ruling text. hearing-required marks rulings where the court requires live argument or appearance instead of deciding on the papers; dotted/tentative until docket/calendar confirmation lands.

instrument.notes: Disposition / filed-instrument type derived from the motion/petition CAPTION (case_title + calendar_matter; ruling text is a backup). fee-award = attorney/expert fees & costs award or motion; judgment = a JUDGMENT instrument (notice of entry of judgment, default judgment, judgment of dismissal, consent/entry of judgment) but NOT motion-types that merely contain the word (judgment on the pleadings, summary judgment, substituted judgment); stipulation = a stipulation / stipulation-and-order. TITLES are the tentative-tier signal; the docket ROA/disposition is the eventual source of truth.

status.notes: Party status tags; propria-persona and vexatious-litigant are INDEPENDENT (only a self-represented person can be DECLARED vexatious, but once declared they stay vexatious even when later represented by counsel). propria-persona = self-represented appearance (in propria persona / in pro per / pro se); source of truth is the docket Parties/Attorneys tab (a party with NO attorney / 'PRO PER'), tentative-text the dotted fallback. vexatious-litigant has a STRICT, special provenance — NEVER inferred from tentative-ruling text: (1) on the current Judicial Council Vexatious Litigant List -> source=judicial_council, SOLID/authoritative (the live 'in effect' roster; SF rows match by case_number); (2) a docket court order declaring them vexatious but NOT on the current list -> rendered TENTATIVE/DOTTED even though it is docket-sourced, because the order may have been vacated/expired/unreported and may no longer be in effect; (3) a tentative-ruling mention is NOT a valid source at all.
